questions.q: In which Indian state did the Malayalam New Wave movement of realist art cinema emerge?
questions.explanation: The Malayalam New Wave was a movement of realist art cinema that emerged in Kerala from the early 1970s.

questions.q: Which 1972 film is widely regarded as the work that catalysed the Malayalam New Wave?
questions.explanation: Swayamvaram (1972) is credited with catalysing the Malayalam New Wave movement.

questions.q: Who directed the 1972 film Swayamvaram, a foundational work of the Malayalam New Wave?
questions.explanation: Swayamvaram (1972) was directed by Adoor Gopalakrishnan, his debut feature.

questions.q: From roughly which decade did the Malayalam New Wave movement begin to emerge in Kerala?
questions.explanation: The Malayalam New Wave emerged from the early 1970s.

questions.q: Which acclaimed filmmaker, associated with the Malayalam New Wave, directed films such as Kanchana Sita and Thampu?
questions.explanation: G. Aravindan, a leading figure of the Malayalam New Wave, directed Kanchana Sita and Thampu.

questions.q: What broad style of cinema is the Malayalam New Wave best known for?
questions.explanation: The Malayalam New Wave is known for realist, socially conscious art cinema.

questions.q: Which director, known for the experimental film Amma Ariyan, was a key figure of the Malayalam New Wave?
questions.explanation: John Abraham, a key Malayalam New Wave figure, directed Amma Ariyan (1986).

questions.q: In which language were the films of the Malayalam New Wave made?
questions.explanation: As the name indicates, the Malayalam New Wave films were made in the Malayalam language.

questions.q: What kind of recognition did the Malayalam New Wave bring to Malayalam cinema?
questions.explanation: The movement brought Malayalam cinema sustained international recognition at festivals and beyond.

questions.q: Adoor Gopalakrishnan, a central figure of the Malayalam New Wave, co-founded which pioneering film society and cooperative in Kerala?
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan co-founded the Chitralekha Film Cooperative, which supported new Malayalam art cinema.

questions.q: Swayamvaram (1972), the film that launched the Malayalam New Wave, won which top honour at the National Film Awards?
options: Best Feature Film in Malayalam only
Best Feature Film (the national top award)
questions.explanation: Swayamvaram won the National Film Award for Best Feature Film, the top national honour, for 1972.

questions.q: Which institutions are credited with supporting the growth of the Malayalam New Wave in Kerala?
questions.explanation: The Malayalam New Wave was supported by cooperatives and a vibrant film society movement in Kerala.

questions.q: Which Adoor Gopalakrishnan film, centred on a reclusive feudal landlord trapped by his own decaying world, won him the National Film Award for Best Direction?
questions.explanation: Elippathayam (1981) won Adoor Gopalakrishnan the National Film Award for Best Direction and the British Film Institute's Sutherland Trophy.

questions.q: Which cinematographer-turned-director, who shot many Aravindan films, directed the acclaimed Malayalam New Wave film Piravi (1989)?
questions.explanation: Shaji N. Karun, a noted cinematographer, directed Piravi (1989), which won a Camera d'Or special mention at Cannes.

questions.q: Adoor Gopalakrishnan studied film at which institute before launching the Malayalam New Wave?
options: Satyajit Ray Film and Television Institute
Film and Television Institute of India (FTII), Pune
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan studied at the Film and Television Institute of India (FTII) in Pune.

questions.q: Which G. Aravindan film is a near-wordless work following a circus troupe pitched in a Kerala village?
questions.explanation: Thampu (1978) is G. Aravindan's largely wordless film about a circus troupe arriving in a village.

questions.q: John Abraham's 1986 film Amma Ariyan was financed in an unusual way for the Malayalam New Wave. How?
options: Through public donations collected by the Odessa Collective
questions.explanation: Amma Ariyan was funded through public collections organised by John Abraham's Odessa Collective film movement.

questions.q: Which director, known for films like Yavanika and Adaminte Variyellu, was a major figure bridging art and middle cinema in 1980s Malayalam film?
questions.explanation: K. G. George directed acclaimed films such as Yavanika and Adaminte Variyellu within the Malayalam new cinema tradition.

questions.q: G. Aravindan's films grew out of his earlier fame in another art form. In what field was Aravindan first widely known before he became a filmmaker?
questions.explanation: Before films, G. Aravindan was famous as a cartoonist, notably for his long-running comic strip in the Malayalam weekly Mathrubhumi.

questions.q: Before the New Wave's arrival, which 1965 film by Ramu Kariat is often seen as a precursor that won Malayalam cinema its first President's Gold Medal for Best Feature Film?
questions.explanation: Chemmeen (1965) won the President's Gold Medal for Best Feature Film, a notable precursor to the realist Malayalam New Wave.

questions.q: Adoor Gopalakrishnan's Mathilukal (1990) was adapted from a prison-set love story by which famous Malayalam author?
questions.explanation: Mathilukal (1990) was based on Vaikom Muhammad Basheer's autobiographical prison story and starred Mammootty.

questions.q: Which Shaji N. Karun film starring Mohanlal as a Kathakali performer was selected for the Un Certain Regard section at the 1999 Cannes Film Festival?
questions.explanation: Shaji N. Karun's Vanaprastham (1999), starring Mohanlal as a Kathakali artist, was shown at Cannes in the Un Certain Regard section.

questions.q: Adoor Gopalakrishnan's Elippathayam (1981) won which prestigious British award for the most original and imaginative film?
options: The British Film Institute Sutherland Trophy
questions.explanation: Elippathayam won the British Film Institute's Sutherland Trophy for the most original and imaginative film.

questions.q: Piravi (1989), directed by Shaji N. Karun, drew on which real-life political episode from Kerala?
options: The Emergency-era disappearance of student Rajan
questions.explanation: Piravi was inspired by the case of P. Rajan, a student who disappeared in police custody during the Emergency.

questions.q: Which 1975 film by P. A. Backer is regarded as one of the early politically radical works of the Malayalam New Wave?
questions.explanation: P. A. Backer's Kabani Nadi Chuvannappol (1975) was a politically charged work of the radical Malayalam New Wave.

questions.q: Adoor Gopalakrishnan's Mukhamukham (1984) was notable and controversial for its critical treatment of which subject?
questions.explanation: Mukhamukham offered a critical, introspective look at the Communist movement, sparking debate among the Kerala Left.

questions.q: G. Aravindan's Kanchana Sita (1977) is distinctive for retelling part of the Ramayana with which casting approach?
options: Casting tribal Adivasi people in key roles
questions.explanation: Kanchana Sita reimagined the Ramayana by casting members of an Andhra tribal community, with nature evoking Sita.

questions.q: Which Adoor Gopalakrishnan film, an adaptation of a Paul Zacharia novella about a tyrannical master and his servant, starred Mammootty and won the 1993 National Film Award for Best Feature Film in Malayalam?
questions.explanation: Vidheyan (1993), based on Paul Zacharia's novella, starred Mammootty and won the National Award for Best Feature Film in Malayalam.

questions.q: P. N. Menon's Olavum Theeravum (1970) is often cited as an early influence on the Malayalam New Wave chiefly because it did what?
options: Was shot largely on real outdoor locations rather than in studios
Used elaborate studio sets and song sequences
Was the first Malayalam colour film
questions.explanation: Olavum Theeravum broke convention by being shot on real outdoor locations, foreshadowing the realist New Wave.

questions.q: The Malayalam New Wave was strengthened by the formation of which state-run body that produced and promoted quality films in Kerala?
options: The National Film Development Corporation of Karnataka
The Kerala State Film Development Corporation
questions.explanation: The Kerala State Film Development Corporation supported the production and promotion of quality Malayalam cinema.
